series_description:
Faculty workload requirements instituted by
school and departments. Also includes master
report of teaching workload sent to UT
System.

series_title:
Deeds and Documents
Reflecting Legal Title
series_description:
Documentation of ownership of assets such as
land and buildings, including special warranty
deeds, deed destrictions, and amendments to
deeds.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years

series_description:
Documentation reflecting permission granted
to utility companies and other third parties
allowing use of university property for specific
purposes, including property transfer
agreements and maps
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments: Also includes 5.1.001b.

series_title:
Development of UT Dallas
Synergy Park
series_description:
Documents regarding UT Dallas Synergy Park
lease terms, lease agreements, deed
agreements, Synergy Park expenses, floor
plans, etc.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 10 years

series_description:
Records reflecting a deed of gift of land
granted by the Excellence in Education
Foundation to the university.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
Potential or realized private, corporate, or
public agency funding to the institution,
including endowments and trusts. Includes
letters and agreements of gift, copies of
bequest instruments and wills from individuals
or estates, and related documentation and
correspondence.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
UTS Policy, security is open with
restrictions.

series_description:
Surveys soliciting feedback from customers,
clients, or stakeholders, and the statistical data
maintained from the responses.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
See ADR1051 for summary reports
compiled from surveys.

series_description:
Records reflecting a deed of gift of land
granted by the Texas Research Foundation to
the university.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
Audits and reviews performed by or on behalf
of an agency, including the working papers
that support the audit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
The State Auditor's Office retains any
copies of its audits performed on Texas
state agencies.
See ADR1097 for Audit Plan records.

series_description:
Audits and reviews performed within the
university, including the working papers that
support the audit. Includes HIPAA Risk
Assessments and internal audits.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Includes any supporting documentation
created and/or used to justify and support
legislative appropriations requests, such as
Biannual Budget Requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version of
Legislative Appropriation Requests
must be submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program per
13 TAC 3.3(a)(3)(A) and University
Archives. Working files and related
documentation used in creating the
final plan are not subject to archival
review and may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_description:
Complaints and other unsolicted feedback
received from the public concerning the
agency and records pertaining to the
resolution of the complaint.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
Open unless clearly unwarranted
invasion of personal privacy. CAUTION:
If a complaint becomes the subject of
litigation, it must be included in and is
subject to the minimum retention
period of item number ADR1036.

series_description:
Incoming/outgoing and internal leadership,
supervisory, managerial, executive,
administrative, substantive, and other high-
level correspondence in any format pertaining
to the formulation, planning, implementation,
interpretation, modification, or redefinition of
the programs, services, or projects of an
agency and the administrative regulations,
policies, and procedures that govern them.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
administrative correspondence of
cabinet-level administration require
archival review. CAUTION: This record
series and item number ADR1020
should be used only for
correspondence that is not included in
or directly related to another record
series in this schedule. For example, a
memorandum that documents an
appropriations request must be
retained in accordance with ADR1017;
a letter concerning an audit for that
prescribed by ADR1015, etc.

series_description:
Non-administrative incoming/outgoing and
internal correspondence, in any media,
pertaining to or arising from the routine
operations of the policies, programs, services,
or projects of an agency.
comments:
SEE comment to item number
ADR1019. See also ADR1021.

series_description:
Any document that officially initiates, rescinds,
or amends general office procedures.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 2 years

series_description:
Calendars, appointment books or programs
and scheduling, or itinerary records
maintained by staff during business hours that
document appointments, itineraries, and other
activities of agency officials or employees.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the calendars,
appointment, and itinerary records of
cabinet-level administrators require
archival review. CAUTION: A record of
this type purchased with personal
funds but used by a state official or
employee to document his or her work
activities may be a state record and
subject to this retention period. SEE
Open Records Decision 635 issued on
December 1995 by the Atty. Gen.

series_description:
From agency legal counsel or the Attorney
General, including any requests eliciting the
opinions.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include legal
opinions or advice rendered on a
matter in litigation or with regard to
pending litigation, or opinions redered
for Public Information Act Requests.
SEE item number ADR1026, ADR1027
and ADR1036.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Opinions and advice
should undergo review by the
University Archives under the following
circumstances: landmark legal
opinion; cases that change the way an
agency operates; high degree of
inherent legal interest;
involvement significant people or
events; high degree of media
attention; natural resource litigation
(land and /or oil disputes with
statewide impact, water rights, etc.);
monetary judgments exceeding $10
million; cases involving a minimum of
$1 million in state tax revenue; or
records that, in the opinion of the
agency’s legal counsel, contain highly
concentrated, unique, and valuable
information unlikely to be found in any
other source available to researchers.

series_description:
News, press releases, marketing files and
collateral, or any public relations files
maintained or issued by an agency. Includes
print, electronic, audio, and audiovisual
records

Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for records
that are furnished to the public under Public
Information Act (Chapter 552, Government
Code). Also includes withdrawn requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for records
that are excepted under the Public
Information Act (Chapter 552, Government
Code), including records provided to the
legislature under section 552.008.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Charts or diagrams that show the structure of
an agency and the relationships and relative
ranks of its departments and job positions.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only charts showing
the overall arrangement and
administrative structure of the state
agency need to be transferred to the
University Archives. Organizational
charts showing division/department
level detail are not considered archival.

series_description:
Plans and records relating to the process of
planning new or redefined programs, services,
or projects that are not included in or directly
related to other records series in this schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cabinet-level plans,
such as major reorganizations or
changes to activities at the University
need to be included for archival
review. Small changes to programs in
departments or centers do not need to
be submitted to the archivist.

series_description:
Copies of all proposed, withdrawn, emergency,
and adopted rules; open meetings notices; or
any other documents required by law to be
submitted to the Texas Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Drafts of proposed legislation and related
correspondence.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Survey submissions to a comprehensive data
collection system managed by the National
Center for Education Statistics (NCES) under
the US Department of Education. Data
collected from institutions participating in Title
IV federal student financial aid programs. The
annual interrelated survey components
collected over three (3) reporting periods
include: institutional characteristics,
admissions, enrollments, completions,
graduation rates, outcome measures, financial
aid, cost, finance, human resources, and
academic libraries.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years

series_description:
Notes, text, or recordings of speeches, papers,
presentations, or reports delivered in
conjunction with agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations given by or
on behalf of cabinet-level
administrators require archival review.

series_description:
Instructional materials and other records
developed by an agency associated with
training the entities or non-personnel
individuals it serves, including but not limited
to training manuals, course registration, class
rosters, sign-in sheets, syllabuses, course
outlines, and similar training aids used in
external training programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
See PER3020 for internal personnel
training materials.
CAUTION: Does not inclue hazardious
material training records. See
SSR7018.

series_description:
Records created by or on behalf of an agency
in anticipation of or in the adjudication of a
lawsuit. Includes records relating to
administrative investigations filed with the
EEOC, Department of Education Office of Civil
Rights, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
All Statute of Limitations must be met
before file is closed. Review before
disposal.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Opinions and advice
should undergo review by the
University Archives under the following
circumstances: landmark legal
opinion; cases that change the way an
agency operates; high degree of
inherent legal interest;
involvement significant people or
events; high degree of media
attention; natural resource litigation
(land and /or oil disputes with
statewide impact, water rights, etc.);
monetary judgments exceeding $10
million; cases involving a minimum of
$1 million in state tax revenue; or
records that, in the opinion of the
agency’s legal counsel, contain highly
concentrated, unique, and valuable
information unlikely to be found in any
other source available to researchers.

series_description:
Information resources and operational
strategic plans prepared in accordance with
§2054.095, and §2056.002, Government Code.
Includes working files and related
documentation used in creating the final plan.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
For strategic plans of individual
departments or divisions, use
ADR1029.
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version of
Strategic Plans must be submitted to
the University Archivest and the Texas
State Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(C). Working files
and related documentation used in
creating the final plan are not subject
to archival review.

series_description:
Self evaluations and plans documenting
compliance with the requirements of the
Americans With Disabilities Act.

series_title:
Meeting Agenda and Minutes
- Academic Senate, Academic
Council, Handbook of
Operating Procedures
Committee, Committee on
Educational Policy
series_description:
Official agenda, minutes, and documents
submitted at or in preparation for meetings of
Academic Senate, Academic Council,
Handbook of Operating Procedures
Committee, Committee on Educational Policy.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
See ADR1098 for meeting records
related to the Council on
Undergraduate Education, Graduate
Council, Committee on the Core
Curriculum, International Education
Development Committee, and Student
Fee Committee. See Section 6.3
(Research) for review board records
related to research. See ADR1099 for
all other committees. See ADR1046 for
staff meeting minutes and notes.

series_description:
Audio or videotapes of meetings not falling
elsewhere in this schedule and for which the
purpose of the recording is solely for the
review by invited attendees.
comments:
CAUTION: This series should only be
used for recordings of meetings not
falling under another series in this
schedule. For recordings of student
disciplinary hearings, use STR2009. For
recordings of personnel disciplinary
action meetings, use PER1023.
If written minutes are prepared from
recordings, see ADR1044.

series_description:
Notes taken during meetings from which
written minutes are prepared.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Minutes or notes, and supporting
documentation, taken at internal agency staff
meetings.
comments:
NOTE: Notes taken by attendees solely
for personal recollection (for example,
a personal to-do list), are not state
records.

series_title:
Reports and Studies (Non-
Fiscal) - Raw Data
series_description:
Information or data collected and compiled for
the purpose of producing non-fiscal reports.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
SEE ADR1051 for reports produced
from raw data.
CAUTION: Does not include source
documentation used for information or
data included in or directly related to
another records series in this schedule.
See ADR1047

series_title:
Reports to Legislature -
Annual or Biennial
(Narrative)
series_description:
Biennial narrative reports to the governor and
legislature as required by an agency's enabling
statutes, including annual narrative reports if
they are required by statute. Includes working
files and related documentation used in
creating report.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version of
these reports must be submitted to the
University Archives and Texas State
Publications Depository Program per
13 TAC 3.3. The requirement is met by
sending the required copies of the
reports to the Texas State Publications
Depository Program, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.
Working files and related
documentation used in creating the
final report may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_title:
Enrollment Census Reports
(4th and 12th Class Day
Rosters)
series_description:
Attendance reports prepared by faculty on
class census day and used as source
documentation for enrollment reports
submitted to the Texas Higher Education
Coordinating Board.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
Annual, sub-annual, or special reports or
studies on non-fiscal aspects of programs,
services, or projects compiled by agency
personnel, by advisory committees, or by
consultants that are not noted elsewhere in
this schedule. Includes reports distributed
either internally or to other entities.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only reports and
students relating to major changes to
university programs, such as
establishment of a new school or
program and accreditation require
archival review.

series_description:
Quarterly and annual reports on agency
performance measures submitted to executive
and legislative offices.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
See ADR1047 for documentation used
to produce reports.

series_description:
Reports compiled by agency personnel on a
daily or other periodic basis pertaining to
workload monitoring, task completion times,
number of public contacts, and similar
activities
comments:
CAUTION: If reports are used to
document performance measures, see
ADR1047.

series_description:
Policies adopted for inclusion in the Handbook
of Operating Procedures that set out the rules,
policies, and procedures that govern university
programs, services, or projects.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments: See ADR1061 for working files.

series_description:
Working files and related documentation used
in the development of policies included in the
Handbook of Operating Procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
SEE item number ADR1056 for HOP
policies. See SSR1024 for all other
guidelines, standards, or office
procedures.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final rules, policies, and
procedures, are not subject to archival
requirement and may be disposed of
at the expiration of the retention

series_description:
Reports made to the Office of the Attorney
General on an agency's Public Information Act
activities.
comments: i d

series_description:
Final agreement outlining the terms of
settlement of a litigation, mediated
agreement, or other written agreement
settling a dispute between an outside party
and personnel acting on the university's
behalf
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years
legal_citation:
Texas Civil Practice and
Remedies Code ,
Chapter 154.071.

series_description:
Activities of an institution or department's
alumni and may also provide alumni offices
with information on alumni. This series may
include, but is not limited to: memberships
lists with names, addresses, employer names
and addresses, and positions; promotional
materials concerning annual gatherings;
homecoming plans and programs; data on
individual alumni; degree recipient lists;
outstanding alumni lists; student leader lists;
class officer lists; foreign student rosters;
alumni club records; and notes, memoranda,
and related correspondence concerning
general alumni affairs.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
Refer to item number SSR1016 for
records used exclusively for contacting
alumni.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only promotional
materials concerning annual
gatherings, homecoming plans and
programs, and correspondence
concerning general alumni affairs need
archival review. Rosters and names of
alumni are not archival.

series_description:
Records documenting the administration,
rules, and history of awards, endowed chairs,
fellowships, and scholarships awarded to
faculty, staff, students and alumni based on
merit or achievement. Includes: eligibility
terms and selection criteria, award history and
information on funding sources, award
notifications, summary lists of winners, and
biographies of winners.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only institutional
awards merit archival review.
Department-level awards do not need
to be reviewed for archival value.
Refer to ADR1025 for press
releases. See ADR1094 for award
selection committee records.

series_description:
Records created in the planning or
administration of routine events, such as
reports, promotional materials, press releases
and news clippings, photographs, presentation
materials, schedules of speakers and activities,
registration and attendance lists, participant
evaluations, room reservation lists, and
catering services orders.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
SEE ADR1084 for records of special
events. SEE item SSR1001 for Special
Event Risk Assessments.
Use this series in conjunction with
UTDRRS Section 3.1 for financial
records, SSR1008/9 for contracts or
written agreements, and UTDRRS

series_description:
Materials related to the planning and
arrangement of distinctive events not held on
a regular basis or events involving noteworthy
speakers or guests. May include but not
limited to promotional and publicity materials,
press releases and news clippings,
photographs, presentation materials and
handouts, honoraria records, schedules of
speakers and activities, registration and
attendance lists, participant evaluations, and
related documentation and correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments:
NOTE: Department holding the event
is responsible for determining whether
an event falls under ADR1084 or
ADR1083.
Refer to item number ADR1083 for
routine event administration records.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Documents reflecting
the historical significance, content, and
nature of an event or program require
archival review, such as promotional
and publicity materials, press releases
and news clippings, photographs,
presentation materials and handouts,
and schedules of speakers and
activities. Administrative materials and
financial/honoraria records do not
require archival review.

retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
Refer to item number ADR1025 for
press releases. Refer to item numbers
ADR1026 and ADR1027 for Public
Information Requests.

series_description:
Reports and dashboards generated by the
University Data Warehouse for use by the
requesting department. Includes data reports
prepared by other departments for internal
use.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
See ADR1051 for reports produced
from raw data.
CAUTION: Does not include source
documentation used for information or
data included in or directly related to
another records series in this schedule.
See ADR1047.

series_description:
Documentation related to application and
registration of copyright and trademark
materials owned by the university. Includes
but not limited to a copy of the work itself and
the corresponding copyright application,
registration notice, and supplementary
documents.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Consultation records, notes, letters, memos,
emails, reports and other documentation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaint filed with EEO. If matter
becomes a grievance or the subject of
counseling or litigation, or employee is
subject to disciplinary action, the
records are subject to retention in the
appropriate records series. SEE item
numbers ADR1036, PER1022, PER1023,
PER1024, and PER1026.

series_description:
Subpoenas for production of evidence
produced for litigation in which the state
agency is not a party. Includes legal documents
requiring recipient to appear in court to testify,
or to produce records to be used in litigation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 months
comments:
For subpoenas related to litigation in
which the state agency is a party, see
ADR1036.

series_description:
Selection records for institutional faculty, staff,
students and alumni considered for awards,
fellowships, and scholarships based on merit
or achievement. May include: applications;
nomination letters; recommendations;
transcripts; letters of award notification,
acceptance or denial; demonstration of need
documentation; vote tallies; ranking sheets;
and related documentation and
correspondence. Also includes institutional or
individual applications for external awards.
comments:
CAUTION: Records of award affecting
long-term access to privileges may
need to be kept longer. For example,
records documenting the awarding of
emeritus titles should be kept until the
award is rescinded.
CAUTION: This records series
documents the process of selecting an
individual to receive an award. See
PER1020 for records of an employee
receiving an award, incentive, or
tenure.
See ADR1082 for award administration
and history.

series_description:
Records documenting the request for and
response to questions or requests for help
received by divisions or units, such as
information technology.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
The disposal of help desk tickets need
not be documented through
destruction signoffs or in records
disposition logs.

series_description:
Biennial information security plan for
protecting the security of the agency’s
information.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Includes working papers, agency risk
assessment, and audit committee minutes
used to develop the plan, per Texas Internal
Auditing Act requirement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
See ADR1015 and ADR1016 for
individual Audit records not related to
the development of the Audit Plan.

series_title:
Committee Meeting Agenda
and Minutes - Counsel on
Undergraduate Education,
Graduate Council,
Committee on the Core
Curriculum, International
Education Development
Committee, Student Fee
Advisory Committee
series_description:
Official agenda, minutes, and documents
submitted at or in preparation for meetings of
Council on Undergraduate Education,
Graduate Council, Committee on the Core
Curriculum, International Education
Development Committee, and Student Fee
Committee. May include but not limited to:
agendas; meeting minutes; reports; discussion
of research and raw data; working papers; and
related documentation and correspondence.
comments:
CAUTION: Before disposing of records,
confirm with the Office of Institutional
Success and Decision Support that they
have collected the records they need
for accreditation purposes.
CAUTION: Federal or state regulations
may require longer retention periods.
See ADR1041, ADR1043, and ADR1044
for Committee Records of Academic
Senate, Handbook of Operating
Procedures Committee, and the
Committee on Education Policy. See
ADR1099 for all other committees.
See ADR1046 for Staff Meeting
Minutes and Notes.
See Section 6.3 (Research) for review
board records related to research.

series_description:
Records of standing, advisory, and ad hoc
committees and councils made up of members
from a department, a college, a variety of
units, or an office where policies and
procedures are set not specified in another
series in this schedule. May include but not
limited to: agendas; meeting minutes; reports;
discussion of research and raw data; working
papers; and related documentation and
correspondence.
comments:
CAUTION: Before disposing of records,
confirm with the Office of Institutional
Success and Decision Support that they
have collected the records they need
for accreditation purposes.
CAUTION: Federal or state regulations
may require longer retention periods.
See ADR1041 for meeting records
related to Senate, Academic Council,
HOP, and the Committee on
Educational Policy. See ADR1098 for
meeting records related to the Council
on Undergraduate Education,
Graduate Council, Committee on the
Core Curriculum, International
Education Development Committee,
and Student Fee Committee. See
ADR1046 for Staff Meeting Minutes
and Notes. See Section 6.3 (Research)
for review board records related to
research.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only records
documenting the outcome of major
policy decisions of committees in this
series require archival review.

series_description:
Title IX, discrimination, and other complaints
investigated by internal compliance personnel.
Includes investigations and determination of
responsibility, including informal resolution or
appeal.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
For grievances not investigated by
compliance office personnel see
STR5007 for students and PER1026 for
employees.

series_description:
This series documents the records created in
compliance with the Export Administration
Regulations, including international shipment
documents, export licenses, and export license
exceptions.

series_title:
International Traffic in Arms
Regulations Records
series_description:
This series documents the records created in
compliance with the International Traffice in
Arms Regulations, including international
shipment documents, export licenses, and
export license exceptions.

series_title:
Office of Foreign Asset
Control Regulations Records
series_description:
This series documents the records created in
compliance with the Office of Foreign Assets
Control regulations, including license
applications and covered transactions.

Agency level documents authorizing final
disposition of records under a certified records
retention schedule. Records may also include
destruction authorizations (e.g., form RMD
102) approved by Texas State Library and
Archives Commission
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: If destruction authorizations
are maintained as part of ADR2006,
then longer retention period applies.

series_description:
Master versions of all forms used internally
and externally by the agency, including
subsequent revisions to a form or any
associated design or design modification
requests. Also includes periodic listing of all
forms used internally or externally.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
A records retention schedule (i.e., form SLR
105) that identifies the records that are
created and maintained by an agency,
provides the minimum timeframes the records
must be retained, and includes instructions for
their disposition. This series may include
working files and documentation of
certification and approval by the Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
Original is retained for 50 years by the
State and Local Records Management
Division, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.

series_description:
Forms used to track the transmittal of records
to/from onsite and offsite storage areas or a
transfer of physical custody.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Logs or similar records listing dispositioned
records, which may include records series title,
dates of records, and date destroyed or
transferred.
comments:
CAUTION: Disposition can mean
destroyed or transferred.

series_description:
Worksheets or working papers used to capture
records inventory information, including
location tracking and to document existence
and requirements of a records series.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Records management plans and similar

series_title:

Records - Class C
Misdemeanors
series_description:
Class C misdemeanors and unclassified
violations of state law or local ordinance
punishable by fine only (including arrest
reports and citations). Cases in which an
arrest is made or a citation issued, and a law
enforcement agency has certain knowledge of
the pretrial or adjudicated disposition of an
arrested or cited person, and considers the
case to have been cleared by the conviction or
acquittal of the person arrested or cited, by
the dismissal of charges against the person, or
by the entry on the record of a court by a
prosecuting attorney of a nolle prosequi.
Offense and supplemental offense reports;
investigation reports and notes; witness
statements; latent fingerprints; results of
chemical analysis and polygraph tests; crime
scene, mug shot, and other photographs;
laboratory reports; arrest reports (Class C
misdemeanors only); citations; affidavits;
criminal processes; victim impact statements;
subpoenas; and other records of a law
enforcement agency relating and customary to
the investigation of criminal offenses or other
violations of state law or local ordinance.
comments:
Retention Note: Retention periods
date from date of arrest or citation,
and are based on the highest
classification of offense for which a
person is arrested and charged or, if
applicable, the highest classification of
offense for which a person is tried,
whichever the lesser classification.
Copies of documents in offense
investigation records, the originals of
which are maintained by, filed with, or
returned to a court or another state or
local law enforcement agency, need be
retained only as long as
administratively valuable.
Copies of notices or other processes
that provide certain knowledge of the
pretrial or adjudicated disposition to
the law enforcement agency should be
kept with the offense investigation
report for the full retention period.
Records can be destroyed after death
of individual if death occurs before
retention period expires.
CAUTION: See SSR2049 for records
relating to a juvenile.
